title: rosa flexuosa subsp. pugetii
intro: Rosa flexuosa subsp. pugetii, also known as Puget's rose or fragrant rose, is a fragrant, deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Rosa flexuosa subsp. pugetii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Rosa flexuosa subsp. pugetii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
